summary: Markets surged to record highs on Friday as Intel's blowout Q1 earnings ignited a powerful semiconductor rally that lifted the Nasdaq 1.63% and the S&P 500 0.80%, both closing at all-time peaks. Intel soared 23.6% after crushing estimates and raising guidance on surging AI server demand, dragging AMD up 13.9% and pushing Nvidia past the $5 trillion market cap threshold. The Justice Department's decision to drop its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell removed a lingering uncertainty ahead of next week's FOMC decision, though Powell faces questions Wednesday about whether he will resign now that the probe has concluded. Crypto markets held relatively steady with Bitcoin hovering near $78,000 and Ethereum above $2,300 as traders balanced Middle East ceasefire hopes against elevated oil prices. Q1 earnings season is delivering strong beats, with blended S&P 500 growth now tracking 15.1% year-over-year, up from 13% a week ago, while March CPI showed headline inflation accelerating to 3.3% driven by a 21.2% spike in gasoline prices.

signals.title: Intel crushes Q1 earnings, semiconductor rally lifts Nasdaq to record
signals.desc: Intel surged 23.6% on blowout earnings and strong AI server CPU demand, sparking broad chip sector rally with AMD up 13.9% and Nvidia reclaiming $5 trillion market cap.

signals.title: Justice Department drops Fed Chair Powell investigation
signals.desc: DOJ referred criminal probe of Powell to Fed inspector general, removing uncertainty overhang and clearing path for potential leadership transition to nominee Kevin Warsh.

signals.title: S&P 500 earnings growth accelerates to 15.1% in Q1 2026
signals.desc: Blended earnings growth rose from 13% last week as 78% of reporters beat estimates, marking the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth and supporting elevated valuations.

signals.title: Bitcoin consolidates near $78,000 as Middle East tensions persist
signals.desc: Crypto held tight ranges with Bitcoin at key resistance levels and Ethereum above $2,300 as traders weigh ceasefire extension against naval blockade and ongoing Iran tensions.

signals.title: March CPI jumps 0.9% month-over-month on energy spike
signals.desc: Headline inflation accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year as gasoline surged 21.2%, complicating Fed rate cut path and raising stagflation concerns ahead of Wednesday's FOMC meeting.

verdict: Tech-driven bullish momentum persists into the weekend, but elevated oil and inflation readings keep macro risks front and center for Wednesday's Fed decision.
